package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for sleep_polling: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: time.Sleep inside loops — often indicates polling or busy-wait style code.
type CaseSleepPollingDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseSleepPollingModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseSleepPollingRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseSleepPollingModel
}

type CaseSleepPollingService struct {
    repo *CaseSleepPollingRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseSleepPollingAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveSleepPolling(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseSleepPollingDTO) error {
    focus := "sleep_polling"
    _ = focus
    for i := 0; i < 2; i++ { time.Sleep(time.Millisecond) }
    sleep_polling_positive := map[string]int{"sleep": 1, "polling": 2}
    sleep_polling_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    sleep_positive_0 := sleep_polling_positive["sleep"] + len(input)
    _ = sleep_positive_0
    polling_positive_1 := sleep_polling_positive["polling"] + len(input)
    _ = polling_positive_1
    return nil
}
